1.description: OLS and IV peer effects estimates dropping outlier offices. The figure shows estimates of the effects of office peers on the likelihood that a worker participates in her firm's ESPP. The regressions in the panel on the left side replicate the specification from TableIV, column (1) and the regressions in the panel on the right side replicate the specification from TableIV, column (3). Each panel reports the estimated peer effect (δ) andt-statistic on the estimated peer effect for 19 regressions. The samples for each regression are defined by dropping for each firm the office with the highest deviation between the office and firm mean by the identified characteristic. Each panel also displays the estimated peer effect from the corresponding baseline regression (TableIV, column (1) or TableIV, column (3)).Execis an indicator equal to 1 for employees who hold executive positions.Hris an indicator equal to 1 for employees who work in human resources.HOis an indicator equal to 1 for employees who work in “high-information” occupations, as defined in TableVII.Finis an indicator equal to 1 for employees who work in financial occupations, excluding workers in financial firms.Finmarkis an indicator equal to 1 iffinis one or the employee works in marketing. Age and income categorical variables are defined as in TableIII. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
